title: Organize Phone Photos
summary: Sort photos into albums and delete blurry or duplicate images.
learnerPromise: You will be able to sort your photos into albums and clean out the ones you no longer want to keep.
whyItMatters: A well-organized photo library makes it much easier to find the pictures you care about. Deleting blurry shots and duplicates also frees up storage space.
realLifeUses: You are looking for a specific photo and have to scroll through thousands of images
Your phone storage is almost full because of too many photos
You want to separate vacation photos from screenshots and random pictures
You need to quickly find and share photos of a specific person or event
materialsNeeded: A smartphone with a photo gallery app
result: A photo library with at least two new albums and at least ten unneeded photos deleted.
youWillLearn: How to create a new album or folder in your photo gallery
How to select multiple photos and move them into an album
How to identify and delete blurry, duplicate, or unwanted photos
How to use search or auto-grouping features to find photos faster
miniChallenge: Create two photo albums with clear names and delete at least ten photos you no longer want.
steps: Open your photo gallery and scroll through recent photos to get a sense of what you have
Create two new albums with specific names like 'Vacation 2024' or 'Recipes' or 'Family'
Select photos that belong in each album and move them into the correct folder
Go through your camera roll and delete blurry, duplicate, or irrelevant screenshots
Empty the 'Recently Deleted' folder to fully free up the space
completionCriteria: You created at least two named albums with photos sorted into them
You deleted at least ten unneeded photos and emptied the trash or recently deleted folder
commonProblems: Feeling overwhelmed by the number of photos — start with just the most recent month
Not sure which photos to delete — focus on obvious blurs, duplicates, and screenshots first
Accidentally deleting a wanted photo — most phones keep deleted photos for 30 days in a 'Recently Deleted' folder
tips: Start small — organize just one event or one month's worth of photos
Use your phone's built-in search to find screenshots, selfies, or photos of specific people
Many phones suggest duplicates to delete — look for this feature in your gallery app
Back up your photos to cloud storage before doing a big cleanup, just in case
